known_bugs.summary: Real external game run split one docs-only web game into many serial Codex tasks and exceeded the outer timeout; fixed by grouping complete web-game scaffold delivery into one implementation task.
gh pr merge can merge a PR but return non-zero when local worktree cleanup conflicts; GitHubFlow now verifies remote PR state and records merged when GitHub reports MERGED.
Automatic browser verification generated screenshots but failed to find them when output_dir was relative; BrowserArtifactRunner now resolves output_dir before passing screenshot paths.
GitHub Actions did not install project dependencies and lacked Pillow; CI now runs pip install -e ., and pillow is declared as a runtime dependency.
HTTP pause/resume test raced project status and recovery handoff; resume_run now restores project status before starting recovery, and the HTTP test follows the resumed-run handoff contract.
Editable install failed in CI because setuptools detected multiple top-level packages in flat layout; pyproject now declares explicit package discovery include patterns.
Manual testing showed rendered canvas games can still contain gameplay bugs; fixed by requiring __ALCHEMY_GAME_TEST__ and browser gameplay probes for movement, jump, victory, and restart.
Acceptance harness exposed that non-web Node projects were being marked partial by static web artifact checks; fixed by skipping static web verification for node/python/docs profiles.
Ordinary static web apps were previously checked with canvas-game style assumptions; fixed by splitting static web checks from canvas-game rules and adding a semantic web interaction probe.
Manual playtest and acceptance feedback could be uploaded but was not first-class work; fixed by adding feedback file role inference and must-priority feedback requirement extraction.

Detailed acceptance documents could generate requirements but not executable browser scenarios; fixed by adding deterministic CRUD/auth/upload/dashboard scenario planning and scenario_probe browser evidence.

Feedback files could become requirements but had no first-class delivered-run reopen path; fixed by preserving source_role=feedback, routing feedback to Debug Agent tasks, and adding /feedback/reopen with UI support.
Dry-run worker no longer treats the word blocked in natural-language goals as an explicit worker blocker; only explicit dry-run block constraints trigger blocked status.
Projects can now provide repository_path without a GitHub URL; ProjectBrief records provider=local and ContextBundle indexes the local source path.

done_criteria: The manual long-running engineering SOP is represented by a machine-checkable development_cycle report.
Detailed documents and docs-only repositories drive task planning, Codex execution, static checks, browser verification, CI, review, and delivery.
Fresh public docs-only test repository delivery produced a playable original retro platformer artifact.
Generated static GitHub Actions CI passed on the external test PR.
Automatic browser artifact verification passed with screenshots, nonblank check, pixel diff, and no console errors.
External test PR was merged, and merge evidence is represented in delivery_report and development_cycle.
Full repository unit test suite, JSON spec parsing, diff hygiene, and long-running state validation pass.
Canvas-game artifacts expose a deterministic gameplay hook and fail delivery when semantic gameplay evidence is missing or failed.
Gameplay probe status is surfaced in artifact_report, delivery_report, requirement_coverage, development_cycle, and the browser console summary.
Non-web project profiles skip static web artifact verification instead of producing false partial development-cycle results.
Static-web artifacts are verified with generic app-root/control rules instead of canvas-game-only rules.
Automatic browser verification records semantic_probe evidence for ordinary static web apps.
Feedback files are recognized as first-class intake and converted into must-priority requirement deltas.
Detailed acceptance requirements generate deterministic browser acceptance scenarios for CRUD, auth, file upload, and dashboard flows.
Static web browser verification runs scenario_probe evidence and fails testing readiness when generated scenarios fail.
Scenario evidence is surfaced in artifact_report, delivery_report, requirement_coverage, development_cycle, and the browser console summary.
Feedback files preserve source_role=feedback through requirement extraction and task planning.
Feedback-derived requirements route to Debug Agent tasks.
Delivered runs can be reopened with feedback files through the local API and browser console.
Feedback reopen runs record source run, feedback files, and feedback-recovery branch prefix metadata.
Local repository paths are first-class ProjectBrief repository providers without requiring a GitHub URL.
Local and GitHub source modes converge into the same ProjectBrief -> ContextBundle -> TaskGraph -> RuntimeState contract.
A local-only acceptance harness verifies initial delivery and feedback reopen without creating a GitHub repository.
Dry-run workers do not misinterpret natural-language words like blocked as explicit blocker constraints.
Generated acceptance scenarios can be converted into Playwright/Cypress native UI test drafts and surfaced in document-run, delivery, runtime state, and requirement coverage evidence.
Delivery evidence is summarized into a human-reviewable API/UI contract covering final gate, requirements, probes, native tests, GitHub, blockers, next actions, and development-cycle status.
Feedback repair runs produce source-vs-current recovery_comparison evidence covering score, coverage, must gaps, blockers, probes, and CI.
